title: Trainor, the Druggist
content:
Only the chemist can tell, and not always the chemist,
        What will result from compounding
        Fluids or solids.
        And who can tell
        How men and women will interact
        On each other, or what children will result?
        There were Benjamin Pantier and his wife,
        Good in themselves, but evil toward each other;
        He oxygen, she hydrogen,
        Their son, a devastating fire.
        I Trainor, the druggist, a miser of chemicals,
        Killed while making an experiment,
        Lived unwedded.
meaning:
Edgar Lee Masters's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Trainor, the Druggist"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
